segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translations.zh-Hans: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: Why had the neighbours left their farm?

translations.zh-Hans: 邻居们为什么离开了他们的农场？

segments.text: The river, which forms the eastern boundary of our farm, has always played an important part in our lives.

translations.zh-Hans: 构成我们农场东部边界的那条河，一直在我们的生活中发挥着重要作用。

segments.text: Without it, we could not make a living.

translations.zh-Hans: 没有它，我们就无法谋生。

segments.text: There is only enough spring water to supply the needs of the house, so we have to pump from the river for farm use.

translations.zh-Hans: 泉水只够家庭生活使用，因此农场用水必须从河里抽取。

segments.text: We tell the river all our secrets.

translations.zh-Hans: 我们把所有秘密都告诉这条河。

segments.text: We know instinctively, just as beekeepers with their bees, that misfortune might overtake us if the important events of our lives were not related to it.

translations.zh-Hans: 我们凭本能知道，就像养蜂人对蜜蜂那样，如果不把生活中的大事告诉它，厄运也许就会降临。

segments.text: We have special river birthday parties in the summer.

translations.zh-Hans: 夏天，我们会举行特别的河畔生日聚会。

segments.text: Sometimes we go upstream to a favourite backwater, sometimes we have our party at the boathouse, which a predecessor of ours at the farm built in the meadow hard by the deepest pool for swimming and diving.

translations.zh-Hans: 有时我们去上游一处喜爱的回水湾；有时在船屋里聚会，那是农场的一位前主人建在草地上的，紧挨着最深、最适合游泳和跳水的水潭。

segments.text: In a heatwave, we choose a midnight birthday party, and that is the most exciting of all.

translations.zh-Hans: 遇上热浪，我们就选择在午夜举行生日聚会，那是最令人兴奋的。

segments.text: We welcome the seasons by the riverside, crowning the youngest girl with flowers in the spring, holding a summer festival on Midsummer Eve, giving thanks for the harvest in the autumn, and throwing a holly wreath into the

translations.zh-Hans: 我们在河边迎接四季：春天给最小的女孩戴上花冠；仲夏夜举行夏季庆典；秋天感恩丰收；冬天则把冬青花环抛入

segments.text: current in the winter.

translations.zh-Hans: 水流之中。

segments.text: After a long period of rain, the river may overflow its banks.

translations.zh-Hans: 长时间下雨后，河水可能漫过两岸。

segments.text: This is a rare occurrence, as our climate seldom goes to extremes.

translations.zh-Hans: 这种情况很少发生，因为这里的气候很少走向极端。

segments.text: We are lucky in that only the lower fields, which make up a very small proportion of our farm, are affected by flooding, but other farms are less favourably sighted, and flooding can sometimes spell disaster for their owners.

translations.zh-Hans: 幸运的是，受洪水影响的只有低洼田地，只占农场很小一部分；但其他农场的地势没这么有利，洪水有时会给农场主带来灾难。

segments.text: One bad winter we watched the river creep up the lower meadows.

translations.zh-Hans: 有一年严冬，我们眼看着河水慢慢漫上低洼的草地。

segments.text: All the cattle had been moved into stalls, and we stood to lose little.

translations.zh-Hans: 所有牛都已经赶进牛棚，我们不会蒙受多大损失。

segments.text: We were, however, worried about our nearest neighbours, whose farm was low-lying, and who were newcomers to the district.

translations.zh-Hans: 不过，我们担心最近的邻居；他们的农场地势低洼，而且才搬到这个地区不久。

segments.text: As the floods had put the telephone out of order, we could not find out how they were managing.

translations.zh-Hans: 洪水弄坏了电话，我们无法知道他们应付得怎么样。

segments.text: From an attic window, we could get a sweeping view of the river where their land joined ours, and at the most critical juncture, we took turns in watching that point.

translations.zh-Hans: 从阁楼窗口，我们能一览河流经过两家土地交界处的景象；在最危急的时刻，我们轮流监视那里。

segments.text: The first sign of disaster was a dead sheep floating down.

translations.zh-Hans: 灾难的第一个迹象是一只顺流漂下来的死羊。

segments.text: Next came a horse, swimming bravely, but we were afraid that the strength of the current would prevent its landing anywhere before it became exhausted.

translations.zh-Hans: 接着出现的是一匹奋力游泳的马，但我们担心水流太急，它还没找到上岸之处便会力竭。

segments.text: a raft appeared, looking rather like Noah's Ark, carrying the whole family, a few hens, the dogs, a cat, and a bird in a cage.

translations.zh-Hans: 随后出现了一只木筏，看起来很像诺亚方舟，上面载着全家人、几只母鸡、几条狗、一只猫和一只关在笼中的鸟。

segments.text: We realised that they must have become unduly frightened by the rising flood, for their house, which had sound foundations, would have stood stoutly, even if it had been almost submerged.

translations.zh-Hans: 我们明白，他们一定是被不断上涨的洪水吓得过了头，因为他们的房子地基牢固，即使几乎被淹没，也能稳稳屹立。

segments.text: The men of our family waded down through our flooded meadows with boat-hooks, in the hope of being able to grapple a corner of the raft and pull it out of the current towards our bank.

translations.zh-Hans: 我家的男人们拿着船钩，涉水穿过被淹的草地，希望能钩住木筏一角，把它从水流中拖到我们这边的岸上。

segments.text: We still think it a miracle that they were able to do so.

translations.zh-Hans: 直到今天，我们仍认为他们能够成功简直是个奇迹。
